We will begin by loading the packages used for the rendered analysis. The full runtime image also contains R CoGAPS, PyCoGAPS, Scanpy, AnnData, and supporting single-cell packages for optional reproduction runs.

::: {.panel-tabset}

## R

```{r}
#| label: setup-r-packages
#| message: false
library(here)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(knitr)
library(jsonlite)

if (requireNamespace("reticulate", quietly = TRUE) &&
    file.exists("/opt/cogaps-venv/bin/python")) {
  reticulate::use_python("/opt/cogaps-venv/bin/python", required = FALSE)
}
```

## Python

```{python}
#| label: setup-python-packages
from pathlib import Path
import json

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
```

:::

Package | Use
--- | ---
[`here`](https://here.r-lib.org/){target="_blank"} | Build project-relative paths in R.
[`readr`](https://readr.tidyverse.org/){target="_blank"} | Read CSV tables used in the rendered R code.
[`dplyr`](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/){target="_blank"} | Select, filter, summarize, and arrange R analysis tables.
[`tidyr`](https://tidyr.tidyverse.org/){target="_blank"} | Reshape pattern summaries into long and wide forms in R.
[`ggplot2`](https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/){target="_blank"} | Build and customize R visualizations from tidy tables.
[`knitr`](https://yihui.org/knitr/){target="_blank"} | Render R tables in the case-study document.
[`jsonlite`](https://jeroen.r-universe.dev/jsonlite){target="_blank"} | Read run manifests and diagnostic JSON files in R.
[`pandas`](https://pandas.pydata.org/){target="_blank"} | Read, filter, and summarize CSV tables in Python.
[`matplotlib`](https://matplotlib.org/){target="_blank"} | Build Python visualizations.
[`CoGAPS`](https://bioconductor.org/packages/CoGAPS){target="_blank"} | Optional R implementation of CoGAPS for full model reproduction.
[`PyCoGAPS`](https://github.com/FertigLab/pycogaps){target="_blank"} | Optional Python implementation of CoGAPS for the alternate workflow.
[`scanpy`](https://scanpy.readthedocs.io/){target="_blank"} and [`anndata`](https://anndata.readthedocs.io/){target="_blank"} | Optional Python single-cell preprocessing and H5AD handling.

The first time we use a function in prose-heavy sections, we use the `package::function()` form to show where important functions come from.
